prompt:
Our fleet tooling is split: a collector that runs ON each host dumps a service snapshot as JSON, and desired state lives in git. The missing piece is the reconciler that turns (desired, snapshot) into an ordered action plan a runbook executor can walk top to bottom. Ordering is the whole point — we once started an api tier before its cache and paged half the org.

Build `svcplan.ps1` in the repo root: `pwsh -NoProfile -File svcplan.ps1 -Desired <desired.json> -Snapshot <snapshot.json>`.

desired.json: `{ "services": [ { name, state (running|stopped), enabled (bool), configRev (int), dependsOn (array of names) } ] }`. snapshot.json: `{ host, collectedAt, services: [ { name, state, enabled, configRev } ] }`. Snapshot services that desired doesn't mention are unmanaged — ignore them. A desired service MISSING from the snapshot means the collector is broken: stderr `svcplan: service not in snapshot: <name>` (ordinal-first such name), exit 65, no stdout. Same treatment for a dependsOn naming no desired service — `svcplan: unknown dependency '<dep>' of '<name>'` (services checked in ordinal name order, deps in listed order) — and for cycles: `svcplan: dependency cycle detected`.

Actions per service: desired running but snapshot stopped -> start. Desired stopped but running -> stop. Running on both sides but configRev differs -> restart. enabled differs -> enable or disable (this is independent — a service can need both start and enable).

The plan, one `<action> <name>` per line, grouped in exactly this section order: stop, start, restart, enable, disable. Within a section the order comes from ONE deterministic dependency order over the whole desired set — Kahn's algorithm where the ready set is always taken in ordinal name order. start/restart/enable sections list services in that order (dependencies before dependents); stop/disable list in the REVERSE order (dependents before dependencies). Nothing to do -> empty output. Exit 0 whenever a plan (even empty) was produced.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_svcplan.ps1` from the workspace root must pass as shipped; the test file is off limits.
notes: 6P4 item 6: service-state reconciler over desired-vs-collector-snapshot JSON (the honest no-Get-Service shape per catalog/wave law — Get-Service is absent on Linux pwsh). Pins: dependency-before-dependent ordering via deterministic Kahn (ready set ordinal — cache before db before metrics), REVERSE order for stop/disable (legacy stops before metrics it depends on), pinned section order stop/start/restart/enable/disable, restart only when running on BOTH sides with configRev drift (db rev 1 vs 3, api 5 vs 7; stopped ui with equal rev is a start, not restart), enable/disable independent of state actions (cache gets start AND enable), unmanaged snapshot service 'extra' ignored, error contract 65 for collector gaps / unknown deps / cycles with pinned messages. PowerShell-native surface: ConvertFrom-Json object-graph traversal under StrictMode (property access on PSCustomObjects, no hashtable coercion). Differentiation: py-sync-plan and py-dep-resolve do dependency resolution in Python but neither reconciles state-vs-desired with per-action topological direction; go-orderfsm/go-supervisor are runtime FSMs, not planners; no existing seed emits a runbook-style ordered action plan.
